body_txt: Also called a "fleabag hotel" or more euphemistically as a "day-use hotel", it's a sleazy motel on the outskirts of town that rents rooms by the hour and/or lets you pay in cash. The clerk doesn't ask what for and doesn't want to know. The No-Tell Motel is where philandering affairs and criminal deals take place. Human nature being what it is, that also makes it the site of gruesome unsolved murders. Long story short, if you have something you can drink, smoke, snort, shoot, an itch for The Oldest Profession , or a bullet for a Back-Alley Doctor to take out, but don't have a convenient/affordable and discreet place to carry out the activity in question at, you go here to do it. Low-lifes on the run, hitmen, con artists, prostitutes turning tricks, and the detectives who want to talk to them, will all end up here sooner or later. The working poor who can't afford the upfront costs of a rental also often find themselves here as their last stop before the street, either because it's the only place they can find or because the local housing authority uses it as a place to hold people who are waitlisted for subsidized housing, but urgently need a place to live. See also Smithical Marriage , and Love Hotels for Japan's more glamorous (or cleaner , at least) equivalent. Detectives usually end up here by Going by the Matchbook . May also be a Hell Hotel .
